titleStmt.title: SII 26.589: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 589.

summary.p: On a slab set up in the field adjoining the ‘temple of heroes’.

p.hi: Śaka 12[37], Ānanda, Kārttika śu, 15 = 1314 A.D., October 24, Thursday.

summary.p: It states that this inscribed pillar was set up by Mēdara Mallayya's son (name lost) belonging to Jūlapalli, for the merit of his father Malli-śeṭṭi, mother Mallaka, for the merit of his preceptor Gaṁḍumamchchi-ārādhya and for the welfare of his gōtra Yalamaṁchchi-gōtra. Also mentions [Pō]turāju as the son of China Mallayya. The record is damaged at the end.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
A panel depicting two seated figures worshipping a liṅga is engraved at the top of the record. To the right of this panel is written.svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana
śakavarṣaṁbulu 1237 Agunēṭi Ā
saṁdda saṁvvatsara kārttika śu 15 . jūla
palli mēdaramallayya
śeṭṭi tama taṁḍri malli śeṭṭi tamatalli
mallakaku puṇyaṁgānu tama guru
vu gaṁḍu maṁcci Ārādhyulku puṇya
ṁggānu tamagōtraṁ yalamaṁcci
gōtraṁ vāriki puṇyaṁgānu yī śā
sana gaṁbhamu vēyiṁccenu . Ācā
rula paṁppu nānu tama kulaṁ vāri paṁppu
nānu Āceṁdrārkkastāyi Ayivuṁ
ḍḍē Aṭṭugānu śēyiṁccenu śrēyiṁccina
vāṁḍu cinamallayya koḍuku pōturā
juku Ā
mulōnki rāni ya
ni tappukonēṭi ni tappuko
nalēdu .On the reverse of this slab is found engraved a number of liṅga in rows, below which is a panel showing a person standing before a bull with a person seated on it.
Digital edition of SII 26.589 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
